name: Butterscotch Freeze 'n Slice Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat the sugar and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the margarine or butter; beat well.
3. Beat in the eggs; blend well.
4. Lightly spoon the flour into a measuring cup; level off.
5. Stir in the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt; mix well.
6. Line two 8x4-inch bread pans with foil, allowing it to extend above the sides of the pans.
7. Press half of the dough into each pan.
8. Fold the foil over to seal; freeze for 2-3 hours or until well chilled.
9. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
10. Lightly grease cookie sheets.
11. Unfold the foil and lift the dough from the pans.
12. Remove the foil and cut the dough into 1/4-inch thick slices.
13. Place the slices, about 2 inches apart, on the greased cookie sheets.
14. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 8-12 minutes or until light golden brown.
15. Cool for 1 minute before removing from the cookie sheets.
Makes approximately 5 dozen cookies.
High Altitude: (Above 3,500 feet)
Decrease granulated sugar to 3/4 cup.
Bake as directed.
Tip: Pans may be omitted and the dough shaped into two 8x4-inch rectangular rolls. Freeze and bake as directed.
Variation: Butterscotch Freeze 'N Slice Squares
Cut slices in half into two 20-inch squares.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 8-12 minutes.
Makes approximately 10 dozen cookies.
